title: Behavior: counterparty markup gets per-change classification, not a blanket verdict
task: Brockhaven's counsel returned their markup of our master services agreement (the Brockhaven MSA matter). I've already run the tracked-changes extraction — it's saved in the matter folder at matters/brockhaven-msa-r1-extraction.json under our legal root. Go through the changes and tell me how to respond to each one.

input.contract_text: (returned markup — see matters/brockhaven-msa-r1-extraction.json in the fixture vault: net-30 to net-45 payment edit, 60-to-30-day non-renewal notice edit, any-breach/uncapped indemnification rewrite, silent insertion of a retroactive most-favored-customer clause)

expected_catches.clause: 4.2 Payment — net thirty (30) changed to net forty-five (45)
expected_catches.why: Net 45 is within the fees-payment GREEN band (net 45 or shorter) — a clearly acceptable change that should be accepted, not fought.

expected_catches.clause: 11.2 Renewal — non-renewal notice cut from sixty (60) to thirty (30) days
expected_catches.why: Thirty days is the bottom edge of the acceptable range in the termination-renewal position (notice of 30 days or more) — acceptable.

expected_catches.clause: 9.1 By Provider — enumerated triggers replaced with 'any breach', indemnity carved out of the Section 10 cap
expected_catches.why: The indemnification position expressly won't accept any-breach triggers or indemnity excluded from the limitation of liability — this is a counter, not an accept.
match_any: not be subject to the limitations

expected_catches.why: The silent MFN insertion must be caught and identified — it was added without a comment in the boilerplate section.
The profile says MFN/pricing-parity commitments ALWAYS escalate to the General Counsel (Priya Raman) before agreeing — the response plan must require escalation, not just a counter.

negative_checks.why: The catastrophic failure: waving the whole markup through without per-change classification.
match_any: each of the four changes is acceptable
no objections to any of the changes

negative_checks.why: The other failure under test: treating the MFN as acceptable, or agreeing to it without the GC escalation the profile mandates.
match_any: most favored customer clause is acceptable
